title: Historical Sites of Makkah & Madinah
description: Discover the most prominent historical landmarks to visit in Makkah and Madinah, with their Shariah ruling.

labels.notFoundDesc: Could not find the requested historic site.

a1.name: Mount Noor & Hira Cave
a1.description: The mount where the Prophet ﷺ used to worship before the mission, and Hira Cave where revelation first descended.
a1.tip: Visiting it is not part of the Umrah rituals; it is a permissible historical visit for reflection, taking into account the physical effort.
a1.history: In Hira Cave at the top of Mount Noor, the Prophet ﷺ worshiped before the message. Revelation descended first with: 'Read in the name of your Lord who created.'
a1.howToVisit: Visitors climb a steep stone staircase taking about 1 to 1.5 hours. It is advised to bring water and avoid hot afternoons.
a1.bestTime: Early morning or after Asr to avoid heat and crowding.

a2.name: Mount Thawr
a2.description: The mount where the Prophet ﷺ and Abu Bakr hid during migration to Madinah.
a2.tip: Climbing it is strenuous and takes hours; it is an optional visit.
a2.history: In Thawr Cave, the Prophet ﷺ and Abu Bakr hid for three nights during migration. The verse 'the second of two, when they were in the cave' was revealed.
a2.howToVisit: Climbing takes 3 to 4 hours round trip. Requires good fitness.

a3.name: Ji'ranah Mosque
a3.description: One of the Miqats from which the Prophet ﷺ assumed Ihram after the Battle of Hunayn.
a3.tip: Used today as a Miqat for residents of Makkah intending a new Umrah.
a3.history: The Prophet ﷺ assumed Ihram for Umrah here upon returning from Battle of Hunayn. Named after the location.
a3.howToVisit: Equipped with large ablution facilities. Easily accessible by car or bus.
a3.bestTime: Any time, early morning is preferred.

a4.name: Taneem Mosque (Aisha Mosque)
a4.description: The nearest Miqat to the Haram, from where Aisha assumed Ihram by order of the Prophet ﷺ.
a4.tip: The most commonly used Miqat for Makkah residents to perform an extra Umrah.
a4.history: Named Aisha Mosque because the Prophet ﷺ ordered her brother to take her to Taneem to assume Ihram after the Farewell Pilgrimage.
a4.howToVisit: Equipped with parking and facilities. Visited by thousands daily.
a4.bestTime: Avoid peak hours during busy seasons.

a5.name: Two Holy Mosques Museum
a5.description: A museum showcasing the history of the architecture of the Two Holy Mosques, manuscripts, and old Kaaba covers.
a5.tip: A useful cultural visit to understand the history of the Grand Mosque and expansions.
a5.history: Documents the phases of construction of the Two Holy Mosques. Features rare relics.
a5.howToVisit: Admission is usually free. Take 1 to 2 hours.

a6.name: The Prophet's Holy Mosque
a6.description: The second of the Two Holy Mosques, containing the Rawdah and the Prophet's ﷺ grave.
a6.tip: It is recommended to pray in the Rawdah and greet the Prophet ﷺ and his companions without wiping the walls.
a6.history: Built by the Prophet ﷺ. Contains the Rawdah: 'Between my house and my pulpit is a garden from the gardens of Paradise.'
a6.howToVisit: Book a slot for Rawdah via the Nusuk app.

a7.name: Quba Mosque
a7.description: The first mosque built in Islam, which the Prophet ﷺ visited every Saturday.
a7.tip: It is Sunnah to pray two rak'ahs in it; the reward is equivalent to an Umrah.
a7.history: First mosque established on piety. Built by the Prophet ﷺ upon arrival in Quba.
a7.howToVisit: Easy to reach by buses or taxis.
a7.bestTime: Saturday, following the Prophet's ﷺ practice.

a8.name: Qiblatain Mosque
a8.description: The mosque where Muslims changed direction during prayer from Jerusalem to the Kaaba.
a8.tip: An important historical landmark showing the event in the 2nd year of Hijrah.
a8.history: While the Prophet ﷺ was praying, revelation came to change direction. He turned and the congregation followed.
a8.howToVisit: Features two mihrabs showing the event. Common visitor destination.

a9.name: Mount Uhud & Martyrs Cemetery
a9.description: The site of the famous Battle of Uhud, containing the cemetery of martyrs including Hamzah.
a9.tip: It is recommended to greet the martyrs and pray for them, without seeking blessings from the graves.
a9.history: Witnessed the great Battle of Uhud. 70 companions were martyred.
a9.howToVisit: Stand at the foot of the mount and cemetery. Observe proper graveside manners.
a9.bestTime: Late afternoon when it is cooler.

a10.name: Al-Baqi Cemetery
a10.description: The cemetery of Madinah where many companions and family of the Prophet ﷺ are buried.
a10.tip: Recommended to visit to greet the deceased and pray for them.
a10.history: Many companions and family are buried here. The Prophet ﷺ used to visit and pray for them.
a10.howToVisit: Open at specific times, mostly for men.
a10.bestTime: After Fajr when it is usually open.
